commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can make this PR safe by reusing Oracle's existing recoverable ChatGPT URL selection and adding focused regression tests.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a browser navigation target derived from stored session metadata without the existing ChatGPT-only recoverability guard.; Review finding: [P1] Reuse the recoverable ChatGPT URL gate — src/cli/followup.ts:22-24 (sha=6cb093f12d5cb6c608359f7b1c8229682682d043)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds browser-session support for `--followup`, persists and reuses a ChatGPT conversation URL, waits for resumed-thread hydration, skips model selection on resume, and prints richer browser reattach commands on failure.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows the new resolver returns the URL produced from stored browser runtime metadata without applying the current recoverability guard. I did not run a live ChatGPT smoke because this is visible in the diff.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can make this PR safe by reusing Oracle's existing recoverable ChatGPT URL selection and adding focused regression tests.

target.title: feat(browser): resume a ChatGPT thread via --followup (+ reattach guidance on failure)
target.body:
Fixes #236.

## What

Lets you **continue an existing browser (ChatGPT) conversation** across CLI invocations, plus reattach guidance on failure:

- `oracle --followup <browser-session-id> -p "..."` now reopens that session's ChatGPT conversation and submits the prompt as the **next turn** (previously: `✖ --followup requires --engine api`). API followups (`resp_…` / stored API sessions) are unchanged. The model picker is skipped on resume (the thread already has its model).
- Browser failures/timeouts now print the exact reattach commands (`--render` / `--live` / `--harvest`) with the real session slug, so a long Pro run is never silently lost.

## Why

A browser Pro Extended consult can carry hundreds of thousands of tokens of loaded context. A follow-up that reuses that thread costs ~tens of tokens; re-running from scratch re-uploads the whole packet (minutes + real cost). This makes iterative "challenge / drill-down" loops practical.

## The hydration race (worth a look)

Resuming an *existing* thread surfaced a real race (related to the closed #175): after navigating to the conversation the composer briefly reports "ready," the prompt is typed — then the prior history finishes hydrating, React re-renders, and the freshly-typed prompt is **wiped**. A fresh chat never hits this (no history to load); a large resumed thread reliably does.

Fix: after navigating to the resumed conversation, wait for the prior turns to render **and stop growing**, let React settle, then re-confirm the composer before typing — mirroring the existing `recoverConversation` hydration-delay pattern.

## How it works

- `src/cli/followup.ts` — resolves a `--followup` reference: if the stored session is a browser run, returns its conversation URL (from `browser.runtime.tabUrl` / `conversationId`); `resp_` / API sessions fall through to the existing API-followup path.
- `src/browser/index.ts` — navigates to `resumeConversationUrl ?? config.url`, runs the hydration wait, and skips the model picker on resume (local + remote browser paths).
- `src/cli/reattachGuidance.ts` — the reattach-commands message, wired into every browser failure/timeout branch.
- `browserResumeConversationUrl` is plumbed through the run options and persisted in session metadata so `restart` / replay keep working.

## Proof

- `pnpm build` clean (tsgo). **54/54 tests pass** across the new + touched suites (`followup`, `reattachGuidance`, `browser/sessionRunner`, `cli/sessionRunner`).
- **Live, end-to-end:** resumed a real prior browser session (7 prior turns) → composer stuck → submitted to the **same `/c/<id>`** (not a new chat) → Pro answered in-thread with full prior context at **↑28 tokens** (i.e. no packet re-upload) → no crash. The run log shows both `Resumed conversation hydrated (7 prior turns); composer settled.` and `Model picker: skipped (resumed conversation)`.

Branched cleanly off current `main` (1 commit). Initial implementation drafted via Codex; hydration-race fix, review, and live verification done by hand.
target.author: hbruceweaver
